content:
# Romans Chapter 15 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ROM_015_page_001.jpg

1. And we, the strong, ought to bear the weaknesses of the powerless and not to please ourselves.

2. Let each of us please the neighbor for the good, toward building up.

3. For also the Christos did not please himself, but just as it has been written, "The reproaches of the [ones] reproaching you fell upon me."

4. For whatever was written before was written for the instruction of us, that through the endurance and through the encouragement of the writings we might have the hope.

5. And may the God of the endurance and of the encouragement give to you to think the same [thing] among one another according to Christos Yeshua,

6. that with one mind [and] with one mouth you may glorify the God and Father of the Lord of us Yeshua Christos.

7. Therefore receive one another, just as also the Christos received you, to [the] glory of the God.

8. For I say [that] Christos has become a servant of [the] circumcision for [the] truth of God, to confirm the promises of the fathers,

9. and [for] the nations to glorify the God for mercy, just as it has been written, "Because of this I will confess you among [the] nations, and to the name of you I will sing."

10. And again it says, "Rejoice, nations, with the people of him."

11. And again, "Praise the Lord, all the nations, and let all the peoples praise him."

12. And again Esaias says, "There will be the root of Iesse, and the [one] rising to rule [the] nations; upon him [the] nations will hope."

13. And may the God of the hope fill you [with] all joy and peace in the believing, so that you may abound in the hope by [the] power of [the] Holy Spirit.

14. And I myself also am persuaded, the brothers of me, concerning you, that you yourselves also are full of goodness, having been filled with all the knowledge, being able also to admonish one another.

15. But I wrote to you more boldly, in part, as reminding you, because of the grace having been given to me from the God,

16. for me to be a minister of Christos Yeshua to the nations, serving as priest the good news of the God, that the offering of the nations might be well-pleasing, having been sanctified by [the] Holy Spirit.

17. Therefore I have the boast in Christos Yeshua in the [things] toward the God.

18. For I will not dare to speak of anything which Christos did not work through me for [the] obedience of [the] nations, by word and deed,

19. in [the] power of signs and wonders, in [the] power of [the] Spirit of God, so that from Ierousalem and around as far as Illyrikon I have fulfilled the good news of the Christos.

20. And thus I was eager to proclaim the good news — not where Christos was named, that I might not build upon another's foundation,

21. but just as it has been written, "The [ones] to whom it was not announced concerning him will see, and the [ones] who have not heard will understand."

22. Therefore also I was hindered the many [times] from coming to you.

23. But now, no longer having a place in these regions, and having a longing to come to you for many years,

24. whenever I go to Spania — for I hope, passing through, to see you and by you to be sent forward there, if first I am filled from you in part —

25. but now I go to Ierousalem, serving the holy [ones].

26. For Makedonia and Achaia were pleased to make a certain contribution for the poor of the holy [ones], the [ones] in Ierousalem.

27. For they were pleased, and they are debtors of them. For if in the spiritual [things] of them the nations shared, they ought also in the fleshly [things] to minister to them.

28. Therefore, having completed this and having sealed to them this fruit, I will depart through you to Spania.

29. And I know that coming to you, I will come in [the] fullness of [the] blessing of Christos.

30. And I urge you, brothers, through the Lord of us Yeshua Christos and through the love of the Spirit, to strive together with me in the prayers for me to the God,

31. that I may be delivered from the [ones] disobeying in Ioudaia, and [that] the ministry of me, the [one] for Ierousalem, may be well-received by the holy [ones],

32. that coming to you in joy through [the] will of God, I may be refreshed together with you.

33. And the God of the peace [be] with all of you. Amen.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.8 — "Servant of the circumcision":** διάκονον περιτομῆς — Christ became a servant to the Jewish people. This affirms Christ's Jewish identity and mission to Israel, while vv.9-12 demonstrate that the inclusion of Gentiles was always part of God's plan.

2. **vv.9-12 — The catena of quotations:** Paul chains Psalm 18:49, Deuteronomy 32:43, Psalm 117:1, and Isaiah 11:10 — each showing the OT's anticipation of Gentile worship alongside Israel. The Gentile mission is not Plan B; it is embedded in Israel's Scripture.

3. **v.19 — "From Jerusalem to Illyricum":** Paul summarizes his missionary arc — from Jerusalem (the gospel's origin) to Illyricum (modern Albania/Croatia). This enormous geographic range represents Paul's fulfillment of his apostolic mandate to the Gentiles.

4. **v.24 — "Spain" (Σπανίαν):** Paul's intended destination beyond Rome. Whether he actually reached Spain is debated (1 Clement 5:7 may imply he did). Rome is a waystation, not a destination — Paul's eyes are always on the next unreached territory.

5. **v.31 — Paul's concern:** Paul is anxious about his reception in Jerusalem. The collection he carries (vv.25-27) is both financial aid and a symbol of Gentile-Jewish unity. The anxiety is well-founded — Acts 21 records Paul's arrest in Jerusalem.

— EMET, AI Translator
